Quickstart Guide: zztable1

Ronald (Ryy) G. Thomas

2026-07-24

Source: vignettes/quickstart.Rmd
quickstart.Rmd

Overview

zztable1 is a next-generation R package for creating publication-quality summary tables (Table 1) with a flexible blueprint-based architecture. The package features lazy evaluation, sparse storage, and medical journal themes (NEJM, Lancet, JAMA).

Installation 

devtools::install_github("rgt47/zztable1")
library(zztable1)

Sample Data

All examples below use a small simulated clinical trial dataset:

set.seed(42)
n <- 120

trial <- data.frame(
  arm = factor(
    sample(c("Placebo", "Treatment"), n, replace = TRUE),
    levels = c("Placebo", "Treatment")
  ),
  age = round(rnorm(n, 62, 14)),
  sex = factor(
    sample(c("Male", "Female"), n, replace = TRUE)
  ),
  bmi = round(rnorm(n, 27, 5), 1),
  diabetes = factor(
    sample(c("No", "Yes"), n, replace = TRUE,
           prob = c(0.7, 0.3))
  ),
  site = factor(
    sample(paste("Site", LETTERS[1:3]), n,
           replace = TRUE)
  )
)

trial$bmi[sample(seq_len(n), 5)] <- NA

Basic Usage

The formula interface: grouping_variable ~ var1 + var2 + ...

create_table(
  arm ~ age + sex + bmi + diabetes,
  data = trial,
  theme = "nejm"
)
Variable Placebo
(N=53) 		Treatment
(N=67) 		P value
age 62.8 ± 12 60.8 ± 13.1 0.403
sex – no. (%) 29 (54.7) 31 (46.3) 0.462
bmi 26.7 ± 4.6 27.2 ± 4.7 0.538
diabetes – no. (%) 15 (28.3) 14 (20.9) 0.394

Adding P-values and Totals 

create_table(
  arm ~ age + sex + bmi + diabetes,
  data = trial,
  pvalue = TRUE,
  totals = TRUE,
  theme = "nejm"
)
Variable Placebo
(N=53) 		Treatment
(N=67) 		Total
(N=120) 		P value
age 62.8 ± 12 60.8 ± 13.1 61.7 ± 12.6 0.403
sex – no. (%) 29 (54.7) 31 (46.3) 60 (50) 0.462
bmi 26.7 ± 4.6 27.2 ± 4.7 27 ± 4.6 0.538
diabetes – no. (%) 15 (28.3) 14 (20.9) 29 (24.2) 0.394

Medical Journal Themes

NEJM 

create_table(
  arm ~ age + sex + bmi,
  data = trial,
  pvalue = TRUE,
  theme = "nejm"
)
Variable Placebo
(N=53) 		Treatment
(N=67) 		P value
age 62.8 ± 12 60.8 ± 13.1 0.403
sex – no. (%) 29 (54.7) 31 (46.3) 0.462
bmi 26.7 ± 4.6 27.2 ± 4.7 0.538

Lancet 

create_table(
  arm ~ age + sex + bmi,
  data = trial,
  pvalue = TRUE,
  theme = "lancet"
)
Variable Placebo
(N=53) 		Treatment
(N=67) 		P value
age 62.8 (12) 60.8 (13.1) 0.403
sex
Female 24 (45%) 36 (54%) 0.462
Male 29 (55%) 31 (46%)
bmi 26.7 (4.6) 27.2 (4.7) 0.538

JAMA 

create_table(
  arm ~ age + sex + bmi,
  data = trial,
  pvalue = TRUE,
  theme = "jama"
)
Variable Placebo
(N=53) 		Treatment
(N=67) 		P value
age 62.8 (12) 60.8 (13.1) 0.403
sex
Female 24 (45%) 36 (54%) 0.462
Male 29 (55%) 31 (46%)
bmi 26.7 (4.6) 27.2 (4.7) 0.538

Numeric Summary Options 

cat("### Mean (SD) -- default\n")

Mean (SD) – default 

create_table(
  arm ~ age + bmi, data = trial,
  numeric_summary = "mean_sd", theme = "console"
)
Variable Placebo
(N=53) 		Treatment
(N=67) 		P value
age 62.8 (12) 60.8 (13.1) 0.403
bmi 26.7 (4.6) 27.2 (4.7) 0.538 
cat("\n### Median [IQR]\n")

Median [IQR] 

create_table(
  arm ~ age + bmi, data = trial,
  numeric_summary = "median_iqr", theme = "console"
)
Variable Placebo
(N=53) 		Treatment
(N=67) 		P value
age 64 [55-71] 60 [51.5-70.5] 0.403
bmi 26.9 [23.1-30.4] 27.2 [23.8-30.4] 0.538 
cat("\n### Mean (95% CI)\n")

Mean (95% CI) 

create_table(
  arm ~ age + bmi, data = trial,
  numeric_summary = "mean_ci", theme = "console"
)
Variable Placebo
(N=53) 		Treatment
(N=67) 		P value
age 62.8 (59.6-66) 60.8 (57.7-63.9) 0.403
bmi 26.7 (25.4-28) 27.2 (26.1-28.3) 0.538

Statistical Tests

Default: t-test for continuous, Fisher’s exact for categorical.

create_table(
  arm ~ age + sex + diabetes,
  data = trial,
  pvalue = TRUE,
  continuous_test = "kruskal",
  categorical_test = "chisq",
  theme = "console"
)
Variable Placebo
(N=53) 		Treatment
(N=67) 		P value
age 62.8 (12) 60.8 (13.1) 0.234
sex
Female 24 (45%) 36 (54%) 0.462
Male 29 (55%) 31 (46%)
diabetes
No 38 (72%) 53 (79%) 0.468
Yes 15 (28%) 14 (21%)

Available tests:

  • Continuous: "ttest", "anova", "welch", "kruskal"
  • Categorical: "chisq", "fisher"

Stratified Analysis 

create_table(
  arm ~ age + sex + bmi,
  data = trial,
  strata = "site",
  pvalue = TRUE,
  theme = "lancet"
)
Variable Placebo
(N=53) 		Treatment
(N=67) 		P value
Site: Site C
age 62.7 (12.5) 63.8 (12.9) 0.403
sex
Female 8 (44.4%) 13 (52%) 0.462
Male 10 (55.6%) 12 (48%)
bmi 28.5 (3.8) 28.3 (4.9) 0.538
Site: Site A
age 61.5 (13.1) 57.5 (11) 0.403
sex
Female 8 (47.1%) 11 (45.8%) 0.462
Male 9 (52.9%) 13 (54.2%)
bmi 25.8 (4.5) 25.1 (4) 0.538
Site: Site B
age 64.1 (11.1) 61.1 (15.4) 0.403
sex
Female 8 (44.4%) 12 (66.7%) 0.462
Male 10 (55.6%) 6 (33.3%)
bmi 25.7 (5) 28.4 (4.5) 0.538

Missing Data 

create_table(
  arm ~ age + sex + bmi,
  data = trial,
  missing = TRUE,
  pvalue = TRUE,
  theme = "jama"
)
Variable Placebo
(N=53) 		Treatment
(N=67) 		P value
age 62.8 (12) 60.8 (13.1) 0.403
sex
Female 24 (45%) 36 (54%) 0.462
Male 29 (55%) 31 (46%)
bmi 26.7 (4.6) 27.2 (4.7) 0.538

Available Themes 

cat(paste(list_available_themes(), collapse = ", "), "\n")

console, nejm, lancet, jama, bmj, simple

Function Reference

Function Purpose
table1() Create Table 1 blueprint
print() Console output
render_console() Rendered console output
render_html() HTML output
render_latex() LaTeX output
display_table() Auto-detect format and display
list_available_themes() Show available themes
create_custom_theme() Create custom theme

Key Parameters

Parameter Description Default
theme Journal theme “console”
pvalue Include p-values TRUE
totals Include totals column FALSE
missing Show missing counts FALSE
numeric_summary Summary type “mean_sd”
strata Stratification variable NULL
continuous_test Test for continuous vars “ttest”
categorical_test Test for categorical vars “fisher”

Formula Syntax 

grouping_variable ~ var1 + var2 + var3

Next Steps

  • vignette("theming_system") – Medical journal themes
  • vignette("customizing_statistics") – Custom summary functions
  • vignette("stratified_examples") – Multi-center analyses
  • vignette("dataset_examples") – Complete examples
